title: Retrospective Validation Discordance / Adjudication-Ready Record
description: Evidence Foundry E1 Phase 4 (FR-23, PRD OQ-5). TOOL-LOCAL BY DESIGN, same rationale as fixture-corpus.schema.json -- lives under tools/retro-validate/schemas/, never scripts/validate-kb.mjs's wave-2 barrier. Shape for ONE harness-vs-reference-label disagreement, emitted by tools/retro-validate/lib/discordance.mjs#computeDiscordanceRecords from an already-written replay-output.json (never re-runs the engine, never reads raw patient input). Closed property set (additionalProperties:false): caseRef, candidateDigest, engineOutputSet, referenceLabelSet, disagreementClass -- exactly the FR-23 field list, plus corpusId/moduleId/discordanceId/schemaVersion identity/versioning scaffolding. This record is 'adjudication-ready', NOT an adjudication decision: it carries no reviewerId/decision/rationale of its own (those are a human act, composed on top by tools/retro-validate/lib/discordance.mjs#toAdjudicationScaffoldInput before tools/review-record's own `scaffold --role adjudication` verb is invoked). caseRef is a de-identified, corpus-local case identifier only -- this schema carries no clinical-input or patient-identifying content of any kind, and validating against it is never a clinical-validity, safety, or diagnostic-performance claim.

schemaVersion.description: REQUIRED, non-null. Integer discordance-record-shape version, bumped only when this schema's own shape changes.

discordanceId.description: REQUIRED, non-null. Stable, deterministic identifier for this one discordance record within its corpus/candidate run -- derived from (corpusId, caseRef, disagreementClass), never a random/timestamp-derived value (this tool's own determinism posture, FR-19).

corpusId.description: REQUIRED, non-null. The fixture corpus id this disagreement was observed in (schemas/fixture-corpus.schema.json's own `corpusId`, carried through unchanged).

moduleId.description: REQUIRED, non-null. The candidate build's moduleId (lib/replay.mjs#replayCorpus's `candidate.moduleId`), carried through unchanged -- never re-derived.

caseRef.description: REQUIRED, non-null (FR-23 'case ref'). The corpus-local caseId this disagreement belongs to -- a de-identified, corpus-scoped reference only, never patient-identifying content (mirrors schemas/fixture-corpus.schema.json's own case.caseId shape/intent).

candidateDigest.description: REQUIRED, non-null (FR-23 'candidate digest'). The pinned candidate build's registry packDigest (lib/replay.mjs#replayCorpus's `candidate.packDigest`) that produced the engine output being compared -- same sha256:<64 hex> shape schemas/release-registry.schema.json's own packDigest field uses, and the exact same shape tools/review-record's `subjectContentHash` field requires -- this is the P1-T2 canonical adjudication-role scaffold input this record's shape maps onto (see tools/retro-validate/lib/discordance.mjs#toAdjudicationScaffoldInput).

disagreementClass.description: REQUIRED, non-null (FR-23 'disagreement class'). Which single dimension of harness-vs-reference-label agreement this record documents a disagreement in -- one record per (case, dimension) disagreement, never a bundled multi-dimension record (see lib/discordance.mjs's own header for why one-record-per-dimension was chosen over a combined shape).

idSet.description: REQUIRED, non-null (FR-23 'engine output set' / 'reference label set'). The same three id-array shape schemas/fixture-corpus.schema.json#/$defs/referenceLabels and lib/replay.mjs's own per-case output already use -- carried through unchanged, never re-derived or re-abbreviated, so a Workstream A consumer can compare the two sides of a discordance record without a second lookup.
